page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7.xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.7 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance is configured with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio. It does not include local storage or GPU hardware accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks reveal a mixed profile. Single-core processing is moderate, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1876.59 and a gzip compression rate of 27.94 GB/sec, both falling into average tiers. However, multi-core performance is relatively weak, as evidenced by a stress-ng multi-core score of 3865.79 and a Passmark CPU score of 5890.72. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with read-only large block transfers averaging 31.76 GB/sec, though write operations maintain an average tier at 25.49 GB/sec. The CPU supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration, which helps sustain average performance in encryption tests and small-model LLM inference tasks.

Given the dedicated CPU allocation and compute-focused 1:2 memory-to-vCPU ratio, this instance provides a predictable compute environment without the risk of resource contention from shared-core models. However, the low memory capacity and weak multi-threaded scaling limit its suitability for large-scale databases or memory-intensive applications. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, single-threaded processing tasks, basic cryptographic operations, and small-scale microservices where dedicated CPU execution is required but memory demands are minimal.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.7 GHz, paired with 8.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under the KVM hypervisor, this instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a 3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data indicates average single-core performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1876.59 and average static web serving throughput of 6.28 GB/sec, though multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics lean toward the lower tier. Supported by AVX-512 and cryptographic instruction sets, it delivers stable execution for dedicated, compute-bound workloads. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, microservices, and single-threaded processing tasks that require dedicated CPU resources without high memory demands.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 3 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering moderate single-core performance for dedicated workloads.

tagline: A dedicated compute-optimized instance featuring four Intel Xeon cores and eight gigabytes of memory for predictable single-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated Intel Xeon 8369B vCPUs running at 2.7 GHz
8.0 GB of system memory with a 1:2 compute-optimized ratio
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for lightweight web serving and single-threaded microservices
